t_20676099898261487818462350403824885716.readability_explanation: Clear headings and subheadings create a strong visual hierarchy, with bullets consistently used to organize details under each topic. The formatting is uniform and sections are well separated, making key points easy to scan. A minor issue is the dense, multi-clause Overview paragraph, which could be broken up for even easier scanning.
t_20676099898261487818462350403824885716.fluency_explanation: Sentences are generally well-constructed with precise terminology and minimal grammatical issues. However, some sentences—especially in the Overview—are lengthy and slightly wordy, and occasional parentheticals can impede flow. Repetitive attributions (e.g., multiple mentions of the same speaker) add minor redundancy.
t_20676099898261487818462350403824885716.coherence_explanation: Related topics are grouped under clear section headers, and the order mirrors the meeting’s progression from procedural items to audit results, specific issues, and final actions. The Overview sets context effectively before the detailed sections. A small redundancy appears where the consent item information is noted in multiple sections, but it does not disrupt the overall flow.
t_20676099898261487818462350403824885716.tone_explanation: The tone is consistently professional and objective, focusing on reporting discussions without subjective judgment. Language is appropriate for stakeholders and maintains a formal register throughout. Courteous acknowledgments (e.g., thanking staff) are measured and suitable for a business summary.

All other claims preserve the factual meaning of the GT."}, "claims": [{"claim_id": "c_001", "verdict": "accurate", "evidence": ["t_001_p_003", "t_001_p_004"], "issue_type": null, "explanation": "GT states MGO issued a clean opinion on FY2022 financial statements."}, {"claim_id": "c_002", "verdict": "accurate", "evidence": ["t_002_p_001"], "issue_type": null, "explanation": "GT reports about $800 recorded with GASB 87 implementation."}, {"claim_id": "c_004", "verdict": "inaccurate", "evidence": ["t_002_p_001"], "issue_type": "unsupported_by_gt", "explanation": "GT attributes amounts to large t_20676099898261487818462350403824885716.completeness_explanation: {"overall": {"score": 0.871, "total_covered_items": 20, "detail_distribution": {"rich": 13, "adequate": 5, "sparse": 2, "barebone": 0}, "summary": "Overall, the candidate preserves the core operational meaning for most covered items with strong detail. Key audit outcomes, GASB 87 impacts, single-audit timing, community benefit tracking, equipment fund concerns, top employers context, public safety variance explanation, ACFR formatting improvements, minutes approval, and the consent calendar timeline are well captured. Minor gaps include missing legal/tenure context about the auditor, specific mechanics behind equipment fund charge methodology and service-life impacts, the 1\u20132 year delivery timeline for fire apparatus, and omission of the state deficit figures and dependency on anticipated state funding. These omissions are secondary in most cases, resulting in predominantly adequate-to-rich detail levels, with a couple of items trending sparse due to lost explanatory specifics."}, "covered_items": {"topics": [{"topic": "Annual Financial Audit Results Overview", "points": [{"id": "t_001_p_001", "text": "MGO has served as the City's external auditor for seven years, and the City Charter (Section 1106) requires an independent CPA to perform the annual audit.", "detail_score": 0.62, "detail_level": "sparse", "assessment": "The candidate identifies MGO as the city\u2019s external auditor, but omits the seven-year tenure and the City Charter Section 1106 requirement for an independent CPA. Core recognition of the auditor is present, but important legal/tenure context is missing."}, {"id": "t_001_p_002", "text": "The General Operating Fund finished fiscal year 2022 with an operating balance of approximately $17.5 million.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Explicitly states the general fund ended with a $17.5 million operating balance; key figure and context are preserved."}, {"id": "t_001_p_003", "text": "City received clean audit opinion on financial statements with no findings on internal control over financial reporting.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Captures the clean (unmodified) opinion and specifically notes no internal control findings; core message is fully retained."}, {"id": "t_001_p_004", "text": "MGO completed the City's FY2022 financial statement audit and issued a clean opinion with an emphasis of matter for GASB 87 lease implementation. No internal control findings or unrecorded misstatements were identified.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Notes the clean opinion with GASB 87 emphasis, and includes both the absence of internal control findings and unrecorded adjustments. All important elements are preserved."}, {"id": "t_001_p_005", "text": "A Council consent item to accept the ACFR and related audit reports is scheduled for December 13, pending this committee's recommendation.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Specifies the December 13 consent calendar and that it is pending the committee\u2019s recommendation; timeline and condition are preserved."}]}, {"topic": "New Lease Accounting Standard Implementation", "points": [{"id": "t_002_p_001", "text": "Implementation of GASB 87 resulted in recording about $800 million of lease receivables and offsetting deferred inflows on the balance sheet, primarily from large tech-related leases.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "States the ~$800M lease receivable and offsetting deferred inflow and attributes magnitude to major tech leases; core quantitative and causal details are retained."}]}, {"topic": "Equipment Maintenance and Replacement Fund Analysis", "points": [{"id": "t_003_p_001", "text": "Fire apparatus replacement was unusual one-time expenditure, with partial payment made and remaining deliveries expected over 1-2 years.", "detail_score": 0.8, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "Mentions staggered payments for fire apparatus and committed-but-unspent funds, preserving the gist. Omits the 1\u20132 year delivery timeline and the \u2018unusual one-time\u2019 characterization."}, {"id": "t_003_p_002", "text": "Staff will review equipment replacement fund charges to determine if rates should be adjusted for more reasonable balance.", "detail_score": 0.9, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Captures staff\u2019s commitment to review funding levels and charge methodology and consider adjustments; core intent and action are preserved."}, {"id": "t_003_p_003", "text": "The $26 million equipment maintenance and replacement fund balance shown as \"cash and investments\" reflects the fund's share of the City's pooled cash and investments, not a separate account. The pooling approach maximizes returns while maintaining liquidity to meet operational needs.", "detail_score": 0.82, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "Explains that \u2018cash and investments\u2019 reflects a share of pooled city investments rather than separate accounts. Omits the rationale about maximizing returns and maintaining liquidity."}, {"id": "t_003_p_004", "text": "Charges are set to amortize replacement costs (including inflation) over asset lives, aiming for 100% funding without overcollecting. Longer-than-expected vehicle service lives can slow spending and increase balances; staff acknowledged reviewing funding levels and potential charge adjustments could be appropriate.", "detail_score": 0.6, "detail_level": "sparse", "assessment": "Conveys that staff will review charge levels and potential adjustments but omits important mechanics (amortizing replacement costs with inflation, 100% funding aim) and the causal factor (longer service lives increasing balances). Key explanatory details are missing."}]}, {"topic": "Community Benefit Revenue Tracking", "points": [{"id": "t_004_p_001", "text": "Community benefit contributions are tracked in detail within the City's financial system subledgers, but the ACFR aggregates them with other developer fees and does not separately report them.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "States sub-ledger tracking and that ACFR does not separately report these amounts, preserving the main operational details."}, {"id": "t_004_p_002", "text": "Some community benefits are provided directly by developers (e.g., below-market rents, parking, infrastructure) and do not flow through the City; these are not recorded on City ledgers, so external reports may present an incomplete picture of total benefits delivered.", "detail_score": 0.9, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Notes developer-provided benefits that do not flow through city accounts and thus do not appear in statements. While it does not explicitly say external reports are incomplete, the implication and operational meaning are preserved."}]}, {"topic": "Single Audit and Federal Compliance", "points": [{"id": "t_005_p_001", "text": "The Single Audit over federal awards has not yet been completed, but MGO expects to meet the statutory deadline of March 31, 2023 for submission.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Clearly states the single audit is pending and expected to meet the March 31, 2023 deadline; timing and status are retained."}, {"id": "t_005_p_002", "text": "Single audit will include American Rescue Plan Act funds as federal funding subject to compliance review", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Explicitly notes ARPA funds will be included in the single audit; core compliance scope is preserved."}]}, {"topic": "ACFR Document Updates and Improvements", "points": [{"id": "t_006_p_001", "text": "Staff agreed to reformat the ACFR for better electronic readability, responding to feedback that the current print-oriented layout makes side-by-side comparisons difficult in digital form.", "detail_score": 0.9, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Captures the agreement to improve electronic readability and reformat away from a print-oriented layout; core commitment is retained (with additional editorial examples beyond GT)."}]}, {"topic": "Staff Recognition and Transitions", "points": [{"id": "t_007_p_001", "text": "Committee members thanked Jesse Takahashi for service to city as this was his final Finance Committee meeting.", "detail_score": 0.85, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "Conveys thanks and notes Jesse\u2019s departure; it implies a transition but does not explicitly state it was his final Finance Committee meeting."}]}, {"topic": "Top Employers Schedule Clarification and Context", "points": [{"id": "t_008_p_001", "text": "The ACFR's top employers schedule lists only the current top ten, with a cutoff around 500 employees; an employer like LinkedIn may not appear if below that threshold, not necessarily indicating zero employees. The schedule also shows the top ten from ten years ago for historical comparison.", "detail_score": 0.8, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "Explains the top-ten cutoff (~500) and why LinkedIn may not appear. Omits the detail that the schedule also shows the top ten from ten years ago for historical comparison."}]}, {"topic": "Public Safety Expenditure Variance Explanation", "points": [{"id": "t_009_p_001", "text": "A decrease in public safety expenses reflected in the ACFR relates to GASB 68 pension accounting: significant CalPERS investment income reduced entity-wide pension expense, and the public safety pension plan's impact appears specifically in the public safety category.", "detail_score": 0.9, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "States that under GASB 68, investment earnings reduced net public safety expense; while CalPERS is not named, the core cause and impact are preserved."}]}, {"topic": "State Budget Impact Briefing Request", "points": [{"id": "t_010_p_001", "text": "The Chair requested staff provide the Council with information on how the State's projected budget shortfalls (approximately $25 billion, potentially $50 billion next year) could affect City programs that currently rely on anticipated state funding.", "detail_score": 0.78, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "Captures the Chair\u2019s request to analyze potential state budget impacts. Omits the specific shortfall figures ($25B/$50B) and the emphasis on programs reliant on anticipated state funding."}]}, {"topic": "Meeting Procedures and Minutes Approval", "points": [{"id": "t_011_p_001", "text": "Committee approved February 28th, 2022 meeting minutes unanimously via roll call vote", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Reports unanimous approval via roll call for the February 28, 2022 minutes; all key procedural details are retained."}]}], "decisions": []}}

t_20676099898261487818462350403824885716.candidate_claims: {"claims": [{"text": "Macias Gini & O'Connell (MGO) issued an unmodified (clean) audit opinion on the city's FY2021-22 financial statements (ACFR).", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_001"}, {"text": "The city recorded approximately $800 million in lease receivables under GASB 87.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_002"}, {"text": "An offsetting deferred inflow was recorded in connection with the GASB 87 lease implementation.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_003"}, {"text": "The large GASB 87 lease amounts are primarily associated with leases from a major technology firm operating in the city.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_004"}, {"text": "MGO reviewed the GASB 87 lease balances in detail and deemed them reasonably stated.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_005"}, {"text": "MGO identified no internal control findings in the FY2021-22 financial statement audit.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_006"}, {"text": "MGO identified no unrecorded adjustments in the FY2021-22 audit.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_007"}, {"text": "MGO deemed the city's accounting estimates and practices reasonable for FY2021-22.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_008"}, {"text": "The single audit of federal awards for FY2021-22 is pending and expected to be completed by March 31, 2023.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_009"}, {"text": "American Rescue Plan (ARPA) funds will be included in the FY2021-22 single audit of federal awards.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_010"}, {"text": "The general operating fund closed FY2022 with an operating balance of $17.5 million.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_011"}, {"text": "The Equipment Maintenance and Replacement Fund balance is approximately $26 million.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_012"}, {"text": "Some of the equipment fund balance represents committed but not yet paid amounts, such as staged payments for recently ordered fire apparatus.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_013"}, {"text": "The 'cash and investments' line in the ACFR reflects each fund's share of the city's pooled cash and investments rather than separate bank accounts.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_014"}, {"text": "Community benefit contributions are tracked in sub-ledgers within the city's financial system.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_015"}, {"text": "Community benefit contributions are not separately broken out in the ACFR.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_016"}, {"text": "Some developer-provided community benefits do not flow through city accounts and therefore do not appear in the city's financial statements.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_017"}, {"text": "The ACFR's top employers schedule lists only the top ten employers.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_018"}, {"text": "The cutoff for inclusion on the top employers schedule is around 500 employees.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_019"}, {"text": "Pension plan investment earnings recorded under GASB 68 reduced the net public safety expense in FY2021-22 as presented in the ACFR.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_020"}, {"text": "The committee unanimously approved the minutes of the February 28, 2022 meeting.", "claim_type": "decision", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_021"}, {"text": "The committee unanimously recommended that the City Council accept the FY2021-22 ACFR and related auditor reports.", "claim_type": "decision", t_20676099898261487818462350403824885716.readability_explanation: The summary demonstrates excellent visual hierarchy with clear section headings and consistent bullet formatting throughout. The structure is highly scannable with logical divisions between overview, detailed summary, and subsections. Minor deduction for some dense paragraphs in the overview section that could benefit from additional line breaks.
t_20676099898261487818462350403824885716.fluency_explanation: The writing is generally well-crafted with precise language and good sentence flow. However, there are several instances of overly complex sentences, particularly in the overview section with run-on constructions like the opening sentence. Some phrases could be more concise, such as 'deems reasonably stated' instead of 'finds reasonable.'
t_20676099898261487818462350403824885716.coherence_explanation: The summary exhibits excellent logical organization with related topics properly grouped and smooth transitions between sections. The flow mirrors the natural meeting progression from procedural items through audit presentation to specific discussions and final recommendations. All contextual information is appropriately placed before detailed discussions.
t_20676099898261487818462350403824885716.tone_explanation: The tone is consistently professional and objective throughout, using neutral language to report what was discussed without editorial judgment. The formality level is appropriate for stakeholder review, and different viewpoints are presented in a balanced manner. The language maintains professionalism while remaining accessible.

t_20676099898261487818462350403824885716.completeness_explanation: {"overall": {"score": 0.887, "total_covered_items": 19, "detail_distribution": {"rich": 11, "adequate": 8, "sparse": 0, "barebone": 0}, "summary": "The candidate summary demonstrates excellent completeness for covered items. Nearly all covered points are preserved with rich or adequate detail, maintaining operational meaning while providing appropriate context. The summary effectively captures key financial figures, timelines, commitments, and explanations across all topic areas."}, "covered_items": {"topics": [{"topic": "Annual Financial Audit Results Overview", "points": [{"id": "t_001_p_001", "text": "MGO has served as the City's external auditor for seven years, and the City Charter (Section 1106) requires an independent CPA to perform the annual audit.", "detail_score": 0.8, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate identifies MGO as the external auditor and mentions the audit requirement, but omits the specific seven-year tenure and Charter Section 1106 reference. Core operational meaning is preserved."}, {"id": "t_001_p_002", "text": "The General Operating Fund finished fiscal year 2022 with an operating balance of approximately $17.5 million.", "detail_score": 1.0, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate preserves the exact figure ($17.5 million operating balance) and fiscal year context. All important details are retained."}, {"id": "t_001_p_003", "text": "City received clean audit opinion on financial statements with no findings on internal control over financial reporting.", "detail_score": 0.9, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate clearly states the clean/unmodified opinion and no internal control findings. Uses slightly different terminology ('unmodified' vs 'clean') but preserves full meaning."}, {"id": "t_001_p_004", "text": "MGO completed the City's FY2022 financial statement audit and issued a clean opinion with an emphasis of matter for GASB 87 lease implementation. No internal control findings or unrecorded misstatements were identified.", "detail_score": 0.9, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate captures the clean opinion, GASB 87 emphasis of matter, and no internal control findings. The 'no unrecorded misstatements' detail is covered in a different section but the core audit results are well preserved."}, {"id": "t_001_p_005", "text": "A Council consent item to accept the ACFR and related audit reports is scheduled for December 13, pending this committee's recommendation.", "detail_score": 1.0, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate preserves the December 13 consent item scheduling and the committee recommendation requirement. All key details are retained."}]}, {"topic": "New Lease Accounting Standard Implementation", "points": [{"id": "t_002_p_001", "text": "Implementation of GASB 87 resulted in recording about $800 million of lease receivables and offsetting deferred inflows on the balance sheet, primarily from large tech-related leases.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate captures the $800 million figure, GASB 87 implementation, lease receivables and offsetting deferred inflows, and attributes it to a major tech firm. Nearly all important details are preserved."}]}, {"topic": "Equipment Maintenance and Replacement Fund Analysis", "points": [{"id": "t_003_p_001", "text": "Fire apparatus replacement was unusual one-time expenditure, with partial payment made and remaining deliveries expected over 1-2 years.", "detail_score": 0.8, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate mentions fire apparatus with staggered payments but doesn't explicitly call it unusual or one-time. The staged payment concept and timeline are preserved adequately."}, {"id": "t_003_p_002", "text": "Staff will review equipment replacement fund charges to determine if rates should be adjusted for more reasonable balance.", "detail_score": 0.9, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate clearly captures Jesse Takahashi's commitment to review funding levels and charge methodology with potential adjustments. The operational commitment is well preserved."}, {"id": "t_003_p_003", "text": "The $26 million equipment maintenance and replacement fund balance shown as \"cash and investments\" reflects the fund's share of the City's pooled cash and investments, not a separate account. The pooling approach maximizes returns while maintaining liquidity to meet operational needs.", "detail_score": 0.8, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate explains the $26 million balance and pooled investment concept clearly. The rationale about maximizing returns while maintaining liquidity is simplified but the core explanation is preserved."}]}, {"topic": "Community Benefit Revenue Tracking", "points": [{"id": "t_004_p_001", "text": "Community benefit contributions are tracked in detail within the City's financial system subledgers, but the ACFR aggregates them with other developer fees and does not separately report them.", "detail_score": 0.9, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate clearly explains that community benefits are tracked in sub-ledgers but not separately visible in the ACFR, being mixed with developer fee aggregates. Key operational details are well preserved."}, {"id": "t_004_p_002", "text": "Some community benefits are provided directly by developers (e.g., below-market rents, parking, infrastructure) and do not flow through the City; these are not recorded on City ledgers, so external reports may present an incomplete picture of total benefits delivered.", "detail_score": 0.85, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate captures that some developer benefits don't flow through city accounts and therefore don't appear in financial statements. The specific examples and incomplete picture concept are simplified but the core meaning is preserved."}]}, {"topic": "Single Audit and Federal Compliance", "points": [{"id": "t_005_p_001", "text": "The Single Audit over federal awards has not yet been completed, but MGO expects to meet the statutory deadline of March 31, 2023 for submission.", "detail_score": 1.0, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate preserves the single audit status, March 31, 2023 deadline, and expectation to meet it. All important details are retained."}, {"id": "t_005_p_002", "text": "Single audit will include American Rescue Plan Act funds as federal funding subject to compliance review", "detail_score": 0.9, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate clearly states that ARPA funds will be included in the single audit. The compliance review aspect is implicit but the key operational detail is preserved."}]}, {"topic": "ACFR Document Updates and Improvements", "points": [{"id": "t_006_p_001", "text": "Staff agreed to reformat the ACFR for better electronic readability, responding to feedback that the current print-oriented layout makes side-by-side comparisons difficult in digital form.", "detail_score": 0.85, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate captures the electronic formatting improvement commitment and mentions the print-oriented layout issue. The specific side-by-side comparison difficulty is simplified but the core improvement commitment is preserved."}]}, {"topic": "Staff Recognition and Transitions", "points": [{"id": "t_007_p_001", "text": "Committee members thanked Jesse Takahashi for service to city as this was his final Finance Committee meeting.", "detail_score": 0.9, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate clearly captures the thanks to Jesse Takahashi and notes his departure. The 'final Finance Committee meeting' detail is preserved through context of his service ending."}]}, {"topic": "Top Employers Schedule Clarification and Context", "points": [{"id": "t_008_p_001", "text": "The ACFR's top employers schedule lists only the current top ten, with a cutoff around 500 employees; an employer like LinkedIn may not appear if below that threshold, not necessarily indicating zero employees. The schedule also shows the top ten from ten years ago for historical comparison.", "detail_score": 0.8, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate explains the LinkedIn question and the 500-employee cutoff concept clearly. The historical comparison detail is omitted but the core clarification about the cutoff methodology is preserved."}]}, {"topic": "Public Safety Expenditure Variance Explanation", "points": [{"id": "t_009_p_001", "text": "A decrease in public safety expenses reflected in the ACFR relates to GASB 68 pension accounting: significant CalPERS investment income reduced entity-wide pension expense, and the public safety pension plan's impact appears specifically in the public safety category.", "detail_score": 0.85, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate captures the GASB 68 pension accounting explanation and investment earnings impact on public safety expenses. The CalPERS and entity-wide details are simplified but the core explanation is preserved."}]}, {"topic": "State Budget Impact Briefing Request", "points": [{"id": "t_010_p_001", "text": "The Chair requested staff provide the Council with information on how the State's projected budget shortfalls (approximately $25 billion, potentially $50 billion next year) could affect City programs that currently rely on anticipated state funding.", "detail_score": 0.75, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate captures Chair Lieber's request for state budget impact analysis but omits the specific dollar figures ($25 billion, potentially $50 billion). The core request and concern about city program impacts is preserved."}]}, {"topic": "Meeting Procedures and Minutes Approval", "points": [{"id": "t_011_p_001", "text": "Committee approved February 28th, 2022 meeting minutes unanimously via roll call vote", "detail_score": 1.0, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate preserves the February 28, 2022 date, unanimous approval, and roll call vote process. All important procedural details are retained."}]}], "decisions": []}}

t_20676099898261487818462350403824885716.candidate_claims: {"claims": [{"text": "Macias Gini & O'Connell (MGO) issued an unmodified (clean) audit opinion on the city's FY2021-22 financial statements (ACFR).", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_001"}, {"text": "The city recorded approximately $800 million in lease receivables under GASB 87.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_002"}, {"text": "An offsetting deferred inflow was recorded in connection with the GASB 87 lease implementation.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_003"}, {"text": "The large GASB 87 lease amounts are primarily associated with leases from a major technology firm operating in the city.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_004"}, {"text": "MGO reviewed the GASB 87 lease balances in detail and deemed them reasonably stated.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_005"}, {"text": "MGO identified no internal control findings in the FY2021-22 financial statement audit.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_006"}, {"text": "MGO identified no unrecorded adjustments in the FY2021-22 audit.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_007"}, {"text": "MGO deemed the city's accounting estimates and practices reasonable for FY2021-22.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_008"}, {"text": "The single audit of federal awards for FY2021-22 is pending and expected to be completed by March 31, 2023.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_009"}, {"text": "American Rescue Plan (ARPA) funds will be included in the FY2021-22 single audit of federal awards.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_010"}, {"text": "The general operating fund closed FY2022 with an operating balance of $17.5 million.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_011"}, {"text": "The Equipment Maintenance and Replacement Fund balance is approximately $26 million.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_012"}, {"text": "Some of the equipment fund balance represents committed but not yet paid amounts, such as staged payments for recently ordered fire apparatus.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_013"}, {"text": "The 'cash and investments' line in the ACFR reflects each fund's share of the city's pooled cash and investments rather than separate bank accounts.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_014"}, {"text": "Community benefit contributions are tracked in sub-ledgers within the city's financial system.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_015"}, {"text": "Community benefit contributions are not separately broken out in the ACFR.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_016"}, {"text": "Some developer-provided community benefits do not flow through city accounts and therefore do not appear in the city's financial statements.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_017"}, {"text": "The ACFR's top employers schedule lists only the top ten employers.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_018"}, {"text": "The cutoff for inclusion on the top employers schedule is around 500 employees.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_019"}, {"text": "Pension plan investment earnings recorded under GASB 68 reduced the net public safety expense in FY2021-22 as presented in the ACFR.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_020"}, {"text": "The committee unanimously approved the minutes of the February 28, 2022 meeting.", "claim_type": "decision", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_021"}, {"text": "The committee unanimously recommended that the City Council accept the FY2021-22 ACFR and related auditor reports.", "claim_type": "decision", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_022"}, {"text": "The ACFR will appear as a consent item on the December 13 City Council agenda following the committee recommendation.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_023"}, {"text": "Staff will review the equipment replacement fund funding levels and charge methodology and report back to the committee with potential adjustments.", "claim_type": "action_item", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_024"}, {"text": "Staff will change the ACFR terminology from 'citizens' to 'residents.'", "claim_type": "action_item", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_025"}, {"text": "Staff will add the Public Safety Advisory Board to the ACFR advisory body list.", "claim_type": "action_item", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_026"}, {"text": "Staff will pursue improved electronic formatting for future ACFRs to aid review.", "claim_type": "action_item", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_027"}, {"text": "Chair Sally Lieber requested staff analysis of how projected state budget shortfalls could affect the city's financial commitments.",
